Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:38 ET
Massive index-puts single-name calls divergence signals institutional dispersion hedging pre-FOMC
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence (index 2.06 vs single-name 0.94) indicates institutions hedging concentrated equity positions ahead of mega-cap earnings and FOMC.
  • 2. Negative SPY gamma at -$504M amplifies directional moves while QQQ remains protected with +$1.7B positive gamma — creating unusual ETF hedging dynamics.
  • 3. NVDA call blocks into backwardation setup suggests vol buyers positioning for earnings volatility despite technical overbought conditions.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedging pre-events

The most striking development is the extreme divergence between index puts and single-name calls, with ETF P/C ratio spiking to 2.06 while individual stock P/C drops to 0.94 — the widest spread signaling institutional dispersion hedging into next week's mega-cap earnings cluster. This pattern typically emerges when funds hold concentrated tech positions but want broad market protection, particularly relevant given MSFT, META, GOOG, AMZN, and AAPL all reporting within five days of FOMC. The timing coincides with Millennium's aggressive Q4 additions to MSFT (+135%) and AMZN (+398%) reversing prior reductions, suggesting large institutional exposure needs hedging. SPY's negative gamma environment at -$504M creates amplification risk precisely when QQQ maintains strong positive gamma at +$1.7B — an unusual ETF divergence that makes broad market hedges more attractive than Nasdaq protection. This explains why massive SPY put blocks dominate flow (200k+ contracts in 701P and 710P strikes) while NVDA call blocks signal directional positioning despite 13 names showing backwardation including NVDA itself. The vol surface confirms elevated near-term fear pricing, yet institutional flow suggests using that expensive protection to maintain equity exposure rather than reducing risk. Low implied correlation at 0.04 reinforces the dispersion opportunity, with single-name vol more efficient than index vol — exactly the environment where concentrated stock pickers hedge at the index level while maintaining individual equity exposure through calls.

Layer 1: Sentiment -6Layer 2: SPY GEX -$504MLayer 3: P/C divergence 2.06/0.94Layer 9: FOMC + earnings cluster

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
